MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-24-2017 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-13-2016 Continued To Apr-17-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KELLY M. MANNION OF MANNION & LOWE, TEL. NO. 1-415-733-0066 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LILIA SHKOLNIKOV OF WILSON ELSER LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-625-9264 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CAL INSURANCE;
ATTY. JULIAN BARDINI, TEL. NO. 1-415-262-8506 APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT KEMPER INSURANCE BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CAL INSURANCE AND ASSOCIATES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication - CAL Insurance's request for judicial notice is unopposed and granted as to Exhibit M, opposed and granted as to Exhibit L, and opposed and denied as to Exhibit K. As for Hackett's objections to Kemper's evidence, objections ## 1 through 10 are sustained; objection # 11 is overruled. Kemper's motion for summary judgment is DENIED. Summary adjudication is denied because, given the court's ruling on the objections, CAL Insurance has failed to support its motion with admissible evidence demonstrating the absence of triable issues as to Hackett's claims. Even if its evidence were admissible, summary adjudication is denied on the independent grounds that CAL Insurance failed to carry its initial burden to show the non-existence of triable issues whether: (1) it breached its promise to provide advice and recommendations regarding coverage; (2) it satisfied its duty of care in advising Hackett or Hale of insurance needs, accurately representing insurance coverage and requirements, analyzing coverage and advising Hackett or Hale after securing coverage; (3) it intentionally misrepresented that there were no other vehicles or insurable interests in the Hackett/Hale household; and (4) whether any such misrepresentation was with malice, fraud or oppression. (See FAC 15-16, 33 & 39; Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850; Teselle v. McLoughlin (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 156, 171-173 [to carry initial burden, moving party must address allegations of material fact alleged in complaint].) Moreover, CAL Insurance failed to carry its initial burden to show the non-existence of triable issues as to whether Hackett and CAL Insurance had a contractual relationship. (See Response to UMF # 4.) Triable issues exist as to whether CAL Insurance promised that the Kemper policy would cover the Jeep without a gap and whether CAL Insurance breached the promise. (AF #15.) Triable issues exist as to whether CAL Insurance satisfied its duty of care to Hackett. (Response to UMF #5; AF # 15, 26, 32-34, 39; Bordenave Decl., 4.) Triable issues exist as to whether CAL Insurance intentionally misrepresented that there were no other vehicles or insurable interests in the Hackett/Hale household. (AF ## 30, 32-33, 39.) The same triable issues preclude summary adjudication of the prayer for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Joseph M.Quinn: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR # 6576 =302/hk
